summary.chief_complaint: Progressive exertional dyspnea, orthopnea, and bilateral leg swelling for 3 weeks
summary.history_of_present_illness: 60-year-old male with ischemic cardiomyopathy (EF 20%) presenting with NYHA Class IV symptoms. Unable to walk 10 feet without dyspnea. Using 4 pillows to sleep. Progressive weight gain of 25 lbs in 3 weeks despite maximum diuretics.
summary.past_medical_history: Ischemic cardiomyopathy (prior MI x2), ICD in place, CKD Stage 3b, Type 2 DM, CABG 5 years ago
summary.medications_on_admission: Carvedilol 25mg BID, Sacubitril/valsartan 97/103mg BID, Spironolactone 25mg daily, Furosemide 80mg BID, Hydralazine 50mg TID, Isosorbide dinitrate 20mg TID, Metformin 500mg BID, Dapagliflozin 10mg daily
summary.physical_examination: BP 88/52, HR 92, RR 28, SpO2 90% on 4L NC. JVP 18cm. S3 gallop. Bilateral crackles to mid-lung fields. Anasarca with 4+ bilateral pitting edema. Cool extremities.
summary.lab_results: BNP: 4500, Troponin: 0.15, Creatinine: 2.8 (baseline 2.0), Sodium: 128, K: 5.2, Lactate: 2.8, Albumin: 2.5, HbA1c: 7.5%, Cardiac index by right heart catheterization: 1.6 L/min/m2, PCWP: 32 mmHg
summary.imaging_results: Echo: EF 15% (was 20%), severe global hypokinesis, moderate MR, severe TR. CXR: Bilateral pleural effusions with pulmonary edema. RHC: Elevated filling pressures, low cardiac output - cardiogenic shock.
summary.hospital_course: Admitted to cardiac ICU. IV milrinone and IV furosemide drip started. Diuresed 15L over 5 days. Inotrope-dependent - unable to wean. Advanced heart failure team consultation. Listed for heart transplant (Status 2). Temporary intra-aortic balloon pump placed. Palliative care consulted for goals of care. Bridged with milrinone for discharge while awaiting transplant.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Acute decompensated heart failure (cardiogenic shock), End-stage ischemic cardiomyopathy, Inotrope-dependent heart failure, Cardiorenal syndrome
summary.discharge_medications: Milrinone continuous IV infusion via PICC (home infusion therapy), Furosemide 120mg BID, Spironolactone 25mg daily, Metolazone 5mg daily PRN, Dapagliflozin 10mg daily, Insulin glargine 20 units (metformin held)
summary.follow_up_instructions: Heart failure clinic weekly. Transplant evaluation ongoing. PICC line care. Daily weights and I/O monitoring. Sodium <2g/day, fluid 1.5L/day restriction.

summary.chief_complaint: Confusion, falls, and decreased oral intake for 4 days
summary.history_of_present_illness: 82-year-old female brought from assisted living facility with progressive confusion, two falls in 4 days, decreased PO intake, and new urinary incontinence. Baseline is independent with mild cognitive impairment.
summary.past_medical_history: Mild cognitive impairment, Osteoporosis, HTN, Chronic low back pain, Hypothyroidism, Polypharmacy (12 medications)
summary.medications_on_admission: Donepezil 10mg daily, Amlodipine 10mg daily, Oxycodone 5mg Q6H, Gabapentin 300mg TID, Zolpidem 5mg QHS, Omeprazole 20mg daily, Levothyroxine 50mcg daily, Furosemide 20mg daily, Potassium 20mEq daily, Tramadol 50mg Q6H PRN, Diphenhydramine 25mg PRN, Lorazepam 0.5mg PRN
summary.physical_examination: BP 108/58 (orthostatic drop to 85/50 standing), HR 68, Temp 37.2°C. Confused, CAM positive for delirium. Dry mucous membranes. No focal neurologic deficits. MMSE 15/30 (baseline 24/30). Unsteady gait with walker.
summary.lab_results: Sodium: 130, Creatinine: 1.6 (baseline 0.9), BUN: 42, UTI confirmed (>100K E. coli), TSH: 8.5 (undertreated hypothyroidism), Vitamin B12: 180 (low), WBC: 12.5K
summary.imaging_results: CT Head: Age-related atrophy, no acute findings. CXR: No infiltrate. Hip/Pelvis X-ray: No fracture.
summary.hospital_course: Delirium workup identified UTI, dehydration, AKI, and anticholinergic/sedative burden as contributors. Treated UTI with ceftriaxone. IV fluids for dehydration/AKI - creatinine normalized. Comprehensive medication review performed: STOPPED oxycodone, zolpidem, diphenhydramine, tramadol, lorazepam (high-risk medications per Beers criteria). Reduced gabapentin, adjusted levothyroxine. Added B12 supplementation. Delirium resolved over 5 days with MMSE returning to 22/30. PT/OT assessment - safe for return to assisted living with supervision.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Delirium (multifactorial: UTI, dehydration, medication-related), AKI (pre-renal, resolved), Polypharmacy with potentially inappropriate medications, Undertreated hypothyroidism, Vitamin B12 deficiency
summary.discharge_medications: Donepezil 10mg daily, Amlodipine 5mg daily (reduced), Acetaminophen 1000mg TID scheduled (replacing opioids), Gabapentin 100mg TID (reduced), Levothyroxine 75mcg daily (increased), Omeprazole 20mg daily, Vitamin B12 1000mcg daily, Calcium/Vitamin D
summary.follow_up_instructions: PCP 1 week. Neurology for cognitive reassessment in 1 month. Recheck TSH in 6 weeks. Fall prevention program. Remove throw rugs. Night lights. Avoid anticholinergic/sedating medications.

summary.chief_complaint: Bloody diarrhea, severe abdominal cramping, and weight loss for 3 weeks
summary.history_of_present_illness: 47-year-old male presenting with 10-15 bloody stools daily, severe abdominal cramping, urgency, tenesmus, and 12-lb weight loss over 3 weeks. Reports prior episode 2 years ago managed with mesalamine.
summary.past_medical_history: Ulcerative colitis (diagnosed 5 years ago, pancolonic), Ankylosing spondylitis, PSC (primary sclerosing cholangitis)
summary.medications_on_admission: Mesalamine 4.8g daily, Certolizumab 400mg monthly (biologic), Ursodiol 300mg TID
summary.physical_examination: BP 108/68, HR 104, Temp 38.1°C. Pale, dehydrated. Diffuse abdominal tenderness without rebound. Hyperactive bowel sounds. Perianal skin intact. Rectal exam: Grossly bloody stool.
summary.lab_results: Hgb: 8.5, WBC: 14.2, Albumin: 2.4, CRP: 45, ESR: 82, Fecal calprotectin: >1800, Stool CDiff: negative, Stool cultures: negative. ALP: 280 (PSC-related), Total bilirubin: 2.1. CMV PCR: negative.
summary.imaging_results: CT ABD: Pancolonic wall thickening with mucosal hyperenhancement. No megacolon (transverse colon diameter 4.5cm). Abdominal X-ray: No toxic megacolon. Flexible sigmoidoscopy: Severe continuous mucosal inflammation with deep ulcerations (Mayo score 3). Biopsies: Active severe colitis, no dysplasia, no CMV inclusions.
summary.hospital_course: Admitted for severe UC flare (Truelove-Witts criteria: severe). IV methylprednisolone 60mg daily started. Mesalamine continued. Certolizumab held. Day 3 assessment: Partial response but still >6 bloody stools/day. Salvage therapy with IV cyclosporine initiated. Good response by day 5 with reduction to 2-3 non-bloody stools/day. Transitioned to oral cyclosporine bridging to vedolizumab. Nutrition optimized. Colorectal surgery consulted for contingency planning.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Severe acute ulcerative colitis flare (steroid-partial response), PSC with mild cholestasis
summary.discharge_medications: Prednisone 40mg daily (taper over 8 weeks), Cyclosporine 5mg/kg/day (bridge to vedolizumab), Mesalamine 4.8g daily, Vedolizumab 300mg IV (scheduled loading doses), Ursodiol 300mg TID, Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (PJP prophylaxis), Omeprazole 20mg daily, Iron infusion scheduled
summary.follow_up_instructions: GI clinic 1 week with cyclosporine levels. Vedolizumab infusions scheduled. Monthly CBC, CMP, cyclosporine levels. Colonoscopy in 3 months for dysplasia surveillance. PSC monitoring with MRCP annually.

summary.chief_complaint: Severe headache, visual changes, and elevated blood pressure at 34 weeks gestation
summary.history_of_present_illness: 30-year-old G2P1 at 34 weeks presenting with severe frontal headache, scotomata, RUQ pain, and BP 178/112. Routine prenatal labs 2 weeks ago showed onset proteinuria. No seizures.
summary.past_medical_history: Gestational hypertension in first pregnancy (no preeclampsia). Otherwise healthy.
summary.physical_examination: BP 178/112 (confirmed bilateral), HR 88, RR 18. Brisk reflexes 3+ with clonus (3 beats). RUQ tenderness. Gravid uterus consistent with 34 weeks. FHR 140s reactive. Bilateral 2+ edema.
summary.lab_results: Platelets: 82K (dropping from 180K 2 weeks ago), AST: 245, ALT: 198, LDH: 890, Creatinine: 1.3, Uric acid: 8.5, Urine protein/creatinine: 5.2, Peripheral smear: Schistocytes present, Haptoglobin: <10, Fibrinogen: 180 (low)
summary.imaging_results: Ultrasound: Fetus appropriate for gestational age. Normal amniotic fluid. Placenta posterior without abruption. BPP: 8/8.
summary.hospital_course: Diagnosed with severe preeclampsia with HELLP syndrome. IV magnesium sulfate started for seizure prophylaxis. IV labetalol for BP control. Betamethasone administered for fetal lung maturity. Decision for delivery after completing steroid course. Emergency cesarean section performed at 34+2 weeks for worsening labs (platelets dropping to 65K). Live male infant 2.1kg, Apgar 7/9, admitted to NICU. Post-delivery: Labs normalized over 72 hours. Magnesium continued 24 hours post-delivery. BP controlled on oral nifedipine.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Severe preeclampsia with HELLP syndrome, Preterm delivery at 34 weeks, Cesarean section
summary.discharge_medications: Nifedipine XL 60mg daily, Ibuprofen 600mg Q6H PRN, Iron supplementation, Prenatal vitamins (continuing for breastfeeding)
summary.follow_up_instructions: OB follow-up 1 week with BP check. BP monitoring at home. Postpartum preeclampsia watch for 6 weeks. Return for headache, vision changes, or BP >160/110. Contraception counseling. Future pregnancy counseling (recurrence risk). Baby aspirin 81mg in future pregnancies from 12 weeks.

summary.chief_complaint: Sudden onset cold, painful, pale right leg for 6 hours
summary.history_of_present_illness: 56-year-old male presenting with acute right lower extremity ischemia - 6 P's present. Reports sudden onset severe pain in right calf progressing to foot, with pallor, paresthesias. History of PAD and smoking.
summary.past_medical_history: Peripheral artery disease, Atrial fibrillation (not anticoagulated - patient preference), CAD with prior stenting, Type 2 DM, Active smoker 40 pack-years
summary.medications_on_admission: Aspirin 81mg daily, Clopidogrel 75mg daily, Atorvastatin 80mg daily, Metformin 1000mg BID, Cilostazol 100mg BID
summary.physical_examination: BP 145/88, HR 92 irregular. Right LE: Pale, cool below mid-calf. No palpable pulses distal to right popliteal. Delayed capillary refill >4 seconds. Decreased sensation in right foot. Motor function intact but painful with dorsiflexion. Left LE: Diminished dorsalis pedis pulse, warm.
summary.lab_results: CK: 1200 (elevated, rhabdomyolysis risk), Creatinine: 1.5, K: 5.0, Lactate: 4.2, D-dimer: elevated, INR: 1.0. Hemoglobin: 13.5, Platelets: 220K.
summary.imaging_results: CTA Lower Extremities: Acute occlusion of right distal SFA to popliteal artery. Chronic atherosclerotic changes throughout. Poor tibial runoff bilaterally. No aneurysm.
summary.hospital_course: Emergent surgical thromboembolectomy with catheter-directed thrombolysis performed. Intraoperative completion angiogram showed restored flow. Started IV heparin drip, transitioned to warfarin. Post-op: Right foot perfusion normalized with palpable DP/PT pulses. CK trended down after hydration. Smoking cessation program. Discussed importance of anticoagulation for AF. Wound vac to surgical site.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Acute limb ischemia (right lower extremity) secondary to cardioembolism from atrial fibrillation, Peripheral artery disease, Rhabdomyolysis (resolving)
summary.discharge_medications: Warfarin 5mg daily (target INR 2-3), Enoxaparin bridge until therapeutic INR, Aspirin 81mg daily, Atorvastatin 80mg daily, Metformin 1000mg BID, Cilostazol 100mg BID, Nicotine patch 21mg daily
summary.follow_up_instructions: Vascular surgery 1 week. INR check in 3 days then weekly. Smoking cessation program. Wound care for surgical site. Supervised exercise program. Return immediately for any pain, color change, or numbness in either leg.

summary.chief_complaint: Progressive muscle weakness, skin rash, and difficulty swallowing for 6 weeks
summary.history_of_present_illness: 52-year-old female presenting with symmetric proximal muscle weakness (difficulty climbing stairs, raising arms above head), heliotrope rash on eyelids, Gottron papules on hands, and progressive dysphagia for solids over 6 weeks.
summary.physical_examination: BP 132/80, HR 78. Heliotrope rash with periorbital edema. Gottron papules over MCPs, PIPs, elbows. V-sign rash on anterior chest. Shawl sign on upper back. Proximal muscle weakness: hip flexors 3/5, shoulder abduction 3/5. Distal strength 5/5. Crackles at bilateral lung bases (suspicious for ILD). Nasal voice quality. Weak cough.
summary.lab_results: CK: 8500, Aldolase: 28, AST: 185, ALT: 120, LDH: 650. ANA: 1:320 speckled. Anti-Jo-1: positive. Anti-MDA5: negative. Anti-Mi-2: negative. Anti-NXP2: negative. ESR: 55. CT-guided muscle biopsy pending.
summary.imaging_results: MRI thighs: Bilateral symmetric muscle edema in quadriceps and hamstrings. HRCT Chest: Bilateral lower lobe NSIP pattern (ground glass with reticulations) - ILD. PFTs: FVC 62% predicted, DLCO 48% predicted. Video fluoroscopic swallow study: Aspiration with thin liquids. CT CAP: No occult malignancy identified. Mammogram: Normal.
summary.hospital_course: Diagnosed with dermatomyositis with anti-synthetase syndrome (Jo-1 positive) with ILD. Pulse IV methylprednisolone 1g x 3 days. Started oral prednisone 1mg/kg. Mycophenolate mofetil initiated. IVIG 2g/kg over 2 days for severe weakness and dysphagia. Swallow therapy with modified diet (nectar-thick liquids). Malignancy screening completed - CT CAP, mammogram, Pap smear, colonoscopy all negative. Muscle biopsy confirmed perifascicular atrophy. Strength improving from 3/5 to 3+/5.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Dermatomyositis with anti-synthetase syndrome, ILD (NSIP pattern), Oropharyngeal dysphagia
summary.discharge_medications: Prednisone 60mg daily (slow taper), Mycophenolate mofetil 1500mg BID, IVIG monthly infusions, Calcium/Vitamin D, Omeprazole 40mg daily, TMP/SMX (PJP prophylaxis)
summary.follow_up_instructions: Rheumatology 2 weeks. Pulmonology 2 weeks with PFTs. Speech therapy for swallowing. Repeat malignancy screening at 6 months and annually x 5 years. Physical therapy. Report any worsening weakness or breathing difficulty.

summary.chief_complaint: Progressive memory loss, getting lost in familiar places, and personality changes for 1 year
summary.history_of_present_illness: 61-year-old male brought by wife reporting progressive cognitive decline over 12 months. Getting lost driving to work, forgetting recent conversations, difficulty managing finances, and uncharacteristic irritability. Wife reports he left stove on multiple times. Acutely brought in for wandering episode - found by police 3 miles from home.
summary.past_medical_history: Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, Mild hearing loss. Family history: Father with dementia onset age 65, paternal aunt with early-onset Alzheimer's.
summary.physical_examination: BP 138/82, HR 72. MMSE: 18/30 (impaired orientation, recall, language). MoCA: 14/30. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Primitive reflexes: Grasp reflex present bilaterally. Gait normal. No parkinsonism. Anosmia on testing.
summary.lab_results: TSH: 2.1, B12: 450, Folate: 12, RPR: nonreactive, HIV: negative, CBC: normal, CMP: normal. Lumbar puncture CSF: Decreased amyloid-beta 42 (320 pg/mL), Elevated phospho-tau (85 pg/mL), Elevated total tau (620 pg/mL). Genetic testing: ApoE4/E4 homozygous.
summary.imaging_results: MRI Brain: Bilateral hippocampal atrophy (Scheltens grade 3), temporal and parietal cortical atrophy. No vascular disease, no mass. Amyloid PET (Florbetapir): Diffusely positive cortical amyloid deposition. FDG-PET: Posterior temporoparietal and posterior cingulate hypometabolism.
summary.hospital_course: Comprehensive dementia evaluation completed. Diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's disease with biomarker confirmation. Started donepezil with plan to uptitrate. Neuropsychological testing: Deficits in episodic memory, executive function, and visuospatial abilities. Occupational therapy home safety evaluation. Social work: Discussed legal planning (healthcare proxy, financial POA), driving assessment. Caregiver support resources for wife. Enrolled in clinical trial registry.
summary.discharge_medications: Donepezil 5mg daily (increase to 10mg after 4 weeks), Lisinopril 20mg daily, Atorvastatin 40mg daily, Melatonin 3mg QHS (sundowning prevention)
summary.follow_up_instructions: Neurology/Memory clinic 1 month. Neuropsych re-evaluation 6 months. Driving evaluation with OT. Support group for patient and caregiver. Legal and financial planning. Home safety modifications. Consider aducanumab clinical trial eligibility.

summary.chief_complaint: Recurrent severe infections, chronic diarrhea, and weight loss for 6 months
summary.history_of_present_illness: 41-year-old female with 6-month history of recurrent sinopulmonary infections (4 courses of antibiotics), chronic watery diarrhea, and 20-lb weight loss. Current admission for pneumonia with empyema. Reports recurrent ear infections as a child.
summary.past_medical_history: Recurrent sinusitis, Bronchiectasis (diagnosed 2 years ago), ITP (resolved), Autoimmune thyroiditis
summary.physical_examination: BP 118/72, HR 90, Temp 38.4°C. Thin-appearing. Sinus tenderness. Decreased breath sounds right base with dullness. No lymphadenopathy (notably absent). No splenomegaly.
summary.lab_results: IgG: 120 (low, normal 700-1600), IgA: <7 (undetectable, normal 70-400), IgM: 35 (low, normal 40-230). IgG subclasses all low. Specific antibody responses to pneumococcal vaccine: Non-protective to 12/14 serotypes. Lymphocyte subsets: Low B cells (CD19: 2%), Normal T cells. Stool studies: Giardia positive.
summary.imaging_results: CT Chest: Right lower lobe consolidation with loculated effusion (empyema). Basilar bronchiectasis bilateral. CT Sinuses: Chronic pansinusitis with mucosal thickening.
summary.hospital_course: Diagnosed with Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID). Chest tube placed for empyema drainage. IV antibiotics (meropenem + vancomycin). Giardia treated with metronidazole. Initiated IVIG replacement therapy (400mg/kg monthly) with first dose in-hospital - tolerated well. Chest tube removed day 4. Immunology counseling regarding live vaccine avoidance, infection precautions, and long-term IVIG therapy.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID), Empyema with pneumonia, Giardiasis, Bronchiectasis, Chronic sinusitis
summary.discharge_medications: IVIG 30g monthly (next infusion scheduled), Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875mg BID x 14 more days, Metronidazole 500mg TID x 7 days (Giardia), Azithromycin 250mg MWF (prophylaxis), Levothyroxine 100mcg daily, Hypertonic saline nebulization BID (bronchiectasis)
summary.follow_up_instructions: Immunology 2 weeks. Pulmonology 1 month. IgG trough level before next IVIG. Avoid live vaccines. Annual CT chest for bronchiectasis. Screen for autoimmune complications and lymphoma annually.

summary.chief_complaint: Severe low back pain radiating to both legs with progressive weakness for 1 week
summary.history_of_present_illness: 73-year-old male presenting with acute-on-chronic low back pain now with bilateral leg weakness, saddle anesthesia, and new urinary retention. Reports 1 week of worsening bilateral sciatica with inability to walk since yesterday. History of lumbar stenosis.
summary.past_medical_history: Lumbar spinal stenosis (L3-L5), Prostate cancer (treated with radiation 5 years ago, in remission), HTN, Type 2 DM, DVT history
summary.medications_on_admission: Gabapentin 600mg TID, Oxycodone 10mg Q6H PRN, Amlodipine 5mg daily, Metformin 1000mg BID, Aspirin 81mg daily
summary.physical_examination: BP 158/90, HR 82. Lumbar tenderness. Bilateral lower extremity weakness: Hip flexion 3/5, Knee extension 3/5, Ankle dorsiflexion 2/5 bilateral. Saddle hypoesthesia. Absent bilateral ankle reflexes, diminished knee reflexes. Reduced anal tone. Bladder scan: 800mL post-void residual.
summary.lab_results: WBC: 8.5K, CRP: 12, ESR: 35. PSA: 0.2 (stable). Creatinine: 1.2. HbA1c: 7.2%.
summary.imaging_results: MRI Lumbar Spine: Severe central canal stenosis at L3-4 and L4-5 with compression of cauda equina. Large L4-5 disc herniation with caudal migration. No epidural abscess or tumor recurrence. Post-void residual confirmed.
summary.hospital_course: Emergent surgical intervention for cauda equina syndrome. L3-5 laminectomy with L4-5 microdiscectomy performed. Post-op: Partial improvement in motor function (hip flexion 4/5, ankle dorsiflexion 3/5). Foley catheter maintained - bladder function slowly recovering. Inpatient rehabilitation initiated. Bowel program started. DVT prophylaxis with enoxaparin. PT/OT with walker training.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Cauda equina syndrome secondary to severe lumbar stenosis with disc herniation, Neurogenic bladder, Bilateral lower extremity weakness (improving)
summary.discharge_medications: Gabapentin 600mg TID, Acetaminophen 1000mg Q8H (replacing oxycodone), Amlodipine 5mg daily, Metformin 1000mg BID, Enoxaparin 40mg daily x 4 weeks, Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily (bladder recovery), Docusate 100mg BID, Senna 2 tabs QHS
summary.follow_up_instructions: Neurosurgery 2 weeks. Acute inpatient rehab admission arranged. Urology for bladder function assessment. Trial of void in 2 weeks. PT/OT intensive. Report any worsening weakness or new bowel/bladder symptoms immediately.

summary.chief_complaint: Severe fatigue, recurrent infections, and easy bruising for 3 weeks
summary.history_of_present_illness: 36-year-old previously healthy female presenting with 3 weeks of progressive fatigue, fevers, recurrent oral thrush, gingival bleeding, and easy bruising. Reports 10-lb weight loss and bone pain (sternum and long bones).
summary.past_medical_history: No significant PMH. No prior cancers or radiation exposure.
summary.physical_examination: BP 108/65, HR 112, Temp 38.6°C, RR 20. Pale, ill-appearing. Oral thrush. Gingival hypertrophy. Multiple ecchymoses on extremities. Petechiae on soft palate. Sternal tenderness. Mild splenomegaly palpable 3cm below costal margin. No significant lymphadenopathy.
summary.lab_results: WBC: 85K (markedly elevated) with 72% blasts on manual differential. Hemoglobin: 6.8, Platelets: 18K. LDH: 2500, Uric acid: 11.2, Phosphorus: 6.8, Potassium: 5.5, Creatinine: 1.6 (baseline unknown). Fibrinogen: 120 (low), PT 16s, D-dimer elevated. Peripheral smear: Numerous blasts with Auer rods.
summary.imaging_results: CXR: No infiltrate or mediastinal mass. CT CAP: Splenomegaly, no lymphadenopathy. Echocardiogram: EF 60% (pre-chemo baseline).
summary.hospital_course: Bone marrow biopsy: 85% cellularity with 78% blasts. Flow cytometry: Positive for CD34, CD13, CD33, HLA-DR, MPO - consistent with AML (M2 by FAB). Cytogenetics: t(8;21) - favorable risk. NPM1 mutated, FLT3-ITD negative. Started tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis (rasburicase, IV fluids, allopurinol). Transfused PRBCs and platelets. Fluconazole for thrush. Induction chemotherapy with 7+3 (cytarabine + daunorubicin) initiated after central venous catheter placement. Tolerated initial days of chemo without major complications. TLS lab values normalized. Neutropenic fever protocol initiated with cefepime on day 3.
summary.discharge_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML with t(8;21), favorable risk), Tumor lysis syndrome (resolved), DIC (improving), Oral candidiasis
summary.discharge_medications: Outpatient not applicable - continued inpatient for neutropenic phase. Ongoing: Cytarabine continuous infusion (days remaining), Cefepime, Fluconazole 400mg daily, Acyclovir 400mg BID, Allopurinol 300mg daily, Ondansetron 8mg Q8H
summary.follow_up_instructions: Patient remains inpatient through count recovery. Day 14 and 28 bone marrow biopsies planned. PICC line care. Neutropenic precautions. HLA typing for potential allogeneic stem cell transplant (though favorable risk - consolidation chemo preferred). Fertility discussion post-induction.
